title: Rudi Portfolio
about_paragraph_1: Hello, I'm Rudi Herouard and I'm 19. I'm currently a first-year student in BTS SIO SLAM (a French technical IT degree program). I live in France, near Fontainebleau/Melun, close to Paris. The only thing that really interests me is computer science.
about_paragraph_2: I have been into computers since I was young. When I get home, I get on my computer to find something to do. I think I want to be a developer, because it seems like the only job that suits me, the only job I feel I can do.
projects_subtitle: Projects I've worked on in my free time and/or for school!
projects_tank_desc: In high school, in our computer club, we built a remote-controlled tank. We built it using an Arduino, 4 motors for the wheels, a speaker for music, and a Bluetooth module for control. It's controlled via Bluetooth with a phone, thanks to the Arduino's C++ code and the Bluetooth module, of course. We finished it right at the last moment. I remember trying to get the speaker working with the Arduino on the bus (hehe).
projects_doom_title: DOOM on the HP Prime Calculator
projects_doom_desc: This is a port of DOOM, the game released in 1993, written in Python, running on the HP Prime calculator. DOOM was originally written in C, so I had to take code from someone very skilled and determined who rewrote it in Python, using the original game files (WAD files). The problem was that this code uses a library only available on computers (pygame), so I had to make quite a few changes to use the calculator's limited libraries. What was demotivating was that I had to make many code changes just to get an initial result (displaying game textures in the buffer), but I succeeded :). The frame rate I got on the actual hardware (not an emulator) was less than 1 FPS :(. If I have the time and motivation, maybe I'll revisit the code to optimize it and gain 1, 2, or even 3 FPS?
contact_text: You can send me an email here: rudi.herouard@gmail.com
